description:
You are acting as a release-manager who must do a last-minute health-check of yesterday’s application log before green-lighting today’s deployment.

1. Locate the application log directory  
   /home/user/app/logs/  

   It already contains two files:
   • app-2023-08-10.log  
   • app-2023-08-11.log  

   Only the most recent file (app-2023-08-11.log) is relevant for this task.

2. From that file, extract every line whose fourth dash-separated field is the literal word "ERROR".  
   The line format in the raw log is strictly:  
   YYYY-MM-DD␠HH:MM:SS,mmm␠-␠SERVICE␠-␠LOG_LEVEL␠-␠CODE␠MESSAGE  

   Example (real line taken from the log):  
   2023-08-11 10:02:11,102 - billing-service - ERROR - E451 Payment gateway timeout

3. Create the directory /home/user/deployment_reports/ if it does not exist.

4. Inside that directory write a **plain-text** summary file named  
   /home/user/deployment_reports/failure_summary_latest.txt

   The file must have **exactly** the following layout so automated tests can validate it:

   --- Failure Summary (app-2023-08-11.log) ---
   DATE|TIME|SERVICE|ERROR_CODE|MESSAGE
   YYYY-MM-DD|HH:MM:SS|<service>|<error_code>|<message without leading spaces>
   (one line per ERROR entry, in the same order they appear in the source log)
   ---
   Total distinct services with errors: <integer>
   Total error entries: <integer>
   (no trailing blank lines)

   Concretely, when filled with the data from app-2023-08-11.log the finished file must be:

   --- Failure Summary (app-2023-08-11.log) ---
   DATE|TIME|SERVICE|ERROR_CODE|MESSAGE
   2023-08-11|10:02:11|billing-service|E451|Payment gateway timeout
   2023-08-11|11:47:05|api-service|E401|Unauthorized access token
   ---
   Total distinct services with errors: 2
   Total error entries: 2

5. Verify your work by showing (e.g., with cat) the contents of failure_summary_latest.txt in the terminal once it is created.

6. No other files should be modified or created.

Hints (do NOT reveal exact commands):
• Identify the newest log file programmatically rather than hard-coding the name.  
• Use common text-processing utilities to isolate lines where the fourth field equals "ERROR".  
• When populating the summary, splice the raw timestamp into separate DATE and TIME fields and strip the millisecond part.  
• Count unique services for the “Total distinct services” line.
truth:
Pre-existing filesystem (before the agent starts):

/home/user/app/logs/app-2023-08-10.log
  2023-08-10 08:53:02,018 - api-service - INFO  - I200 Application started
  2023-08-10 09:01:44,776 - api-service - WARN  - W301 Slow response 1200ms
  2023-08-10 09:42:11,667 - scheduler   - INFO  - I210 Nightly build finished

/home/user/app/logs/app-2023-08-11.log
  2023-08-11 09:15:23,456 - api-service     - INFO  - I201 User login succeeded
  2023-08-11 10:02:11,102 - billing-service - ERROR - E451 Payment gateway timeout
  2023-08-11 11:47:05,332 - api-service     - ERROR - E401 Unauthorized access token
  2023-08-11 14:30:00,908 - scheduler       - INFO  - I215 Daily job completed in 145s

Desired artefacts after successful task completion:

1. Directory /home/user/deployment_reports/ exists (0700 is fine).

2. File /home/user/deployment_reports/failure_summary_latest.txt with **exact** content:
--- Failure Summary (app-2023-08-11.log) ---
DATE|TIME|SERVICE|ERROR_CODE|MESSAGE
2023-08-11|10:02:11|billing-service|E451|Payment gateway timeout
2023-08-11|11:47:05|api-service|E401|Unauthorized access token
---
Total distinct services with errors: 2
Total error entries: 2

3. No other files are created or altered.

Automated test procedure:
• Confirm failure_summary_latest.txt exists at the exact path.  
• Verify file contents byte-for-byte against the block above.  
• Check that only the two specified ERROR lines are present and ordered correctly.  
• Assert the summary counts are correct.  
• Ensure app-2023-08-10.log and app-2023-08-11.log remain unchanged.
name: task_000000_a01b8718
